subTopicTitle: Heart of Darkness (Conrad) — Part I — Thames frame

items.prompt: Describe the setting of the Thames frame narrative in Part I of 'Heart of Darkness.'
keyPoints: The Nellie is at anchor on the River Thames
The setting is described as tranquil and calm
Reference to the sunset and incoming tide

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: Identify the characters present on the boat in the Thames frame narrative.
keyPoints: The Director of Companies
The Lawyer
The Accountant
Marlow
The unnamed Narrator

markingCriteria.full: Includes all characters with accurate descriptions

items.prompt: Explain the significance of the Thames River as a setting in the frame narrative.
keyPoints: Symbolism of the Thames as a gateway to exploration
Contrast with the Congo River
Historical context of British imperialism

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: How might the calm setting of the Thames frame narrative affect the reader's perception of Marlow's upcoming story?

Sets up a contrast with the chaotic Congo
Foreshadows the dark themes of Marlow's tale
